page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.13xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. This instance features 52 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, resulting in 52 vCPUs. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 3.69 GB per core. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor and does not include any local storage or integrated GPU resources.

Network connectivity for the ecs.re6p.13xlarge is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Because it lacks local storage, users must attach external block storage to run operating systems and applications, which introduces a dependency on network-attached storage performance. The dedicated CPU allocation model ensures that all 52 cores are fully assigned to the instance without resource sharing, offering predictable compute performance for workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.

Given the balanced resource allocation of 52 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, this instance is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and medium-scale database hosting. The dedicated core architecture also supports multi-threaded processing tasks, batch computing, and application servers that require consistent CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.13xlarge is an x86_64 server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. It features 52 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, delivering 52 vCPUs alongside 192.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 3.69 GB of memory per core. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance by avoiding resource contention with other tenants. The lack of complimentary public IPv4 addresses requires external networking configuration for public internet access. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading, such as enterprise application servers, web serving, and database hosting.
